meta.description: Signal graveyard & pre-registration log. Every hypothesis MUST be registered here BEFORE testing. Prevents overfitting by tracking the denominator of tried signals.
rules: Register hypothesis BEFORE running any backtest or evaluation
Include specific metric and threshold for success
Update status after testing — never delete failed entries
Statuses: pending (not yet tested), accepted (passed threshold, deployed), rejected (failed threshold), graveyard (was deployed, later removed)

signals.hypothesis: Capping maxOdds at 2.0 improves ROI vs 2.5 or 3.0
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta vs no cap

signals.hypothesis: Filtering out league/market/direction combos with <50% historical hit rate improves ROI
signals.metric: backtest ROI for filtered vs unfiltered bets
signals.result: 1X2 bets filtered out (low hit rates). AH bets kept. 23/24 combos pass at 50% threshold. Only Serie A AH Home fails (49.1%).

signals.hypothesis: Requiring at least one team to be a regression candidate (xG divergence >= 3.0 goals over 10 matches) improves bet quality
signals.metric: backtest hit rate for variance-filtered vs unfiltered
signals.result: Core Ted filter. Regression candidates show higher CLV and hit rates in backtest.

signals.hypothesis: Filtering out matches where a team plays 3+ times in 8 days improves ROI (fatigued teams are unpredictable)
signals.metric: backtest ROI for congested vs non-congested matches
signals.result: Part of Ted's core filter set. Congested matches show degraded model accuracy.

signals.hypothesis: Adjusting match lambdas by 0.90x (crisis) or 0.95x (major) for injured teams improves prediction accuracy
signals.metric: log loss for injury-adjusted vs unadjusted predictions
signals.threshold: lower log loss on injury-affected matches
signals.result: Conservative multipliers deployed. Crisis (3+ key players out): 0.90x, Major (2+ key): 0.95x.

signals.hypothesis: Adjusting defensive regression expectation based on goalkeeper PSxG+/- improves AH hit rate (poor GK = less regression expected)
signals.metric: AH hit rate delta for GK-adjusted vs unadjusted regression

signals.hypothesis: Showing opponent SOS context on picks page helps human decision-making (qualitative — no backtest metric)
signals.metric: qualitative user assessment

signals.hypothesis: Using only post-manager-change xG data (when available) improves prediction accuracy for affected teams
signals.metric: log loss for manager-change matches
signals.threshold: lower log loss vs full-season xG

signals.hypothesis: Auto-registered by test-signal.ts
signals.metric: ROI

signals.hypothesis: Using venue-specific xGD (home team's HOME xGD, away team's AWAY xGD) produces better CLV than blended full-season xGD. This is an architectural decision: Ted ALWAYS uses venue splits. If venue-specific doesn't beat blended, the model should drop the split and simplify.
signals.metric: CLV delta: venue-split model vs blended-xGD model on same bet universe
signals.result: Venue xGD does NOT improve over MI lambdas. Production config (odds≤2.0, edge≥7%, no draws): baseline=1092 bets/+11.0% CLV/+2.9% ROI/+32.1u vs venue-xgd=1007 bets/+10.9% CLV/+1.7% ROI/+17.4u. More restrictive filter (-85 bets) without quality improvement. Unfiltered: 8746 bets at +5.5% CLV vs 9630 baseline. xG-based variance filter skips 54% more matches.
signals.notes: Tested via --venue-xgd flag. Used Understat per-match xG from data/understat-cache/ (6 seasons × 4 leagues). Replaced MI model expected goals with Understat xG in variance filter. The MI model lambdas are already a better expected-goals baseline than raw xG for this purpose.

signals.hypothesis: Attacking overperformance regresses faster (5-10 matches) than defensive overperformance (15-20 matches). A model that uses different lookback windows per side — shorter for attack fade, longer for defense fade — produces higher CLV than symmetric windows.
signals.metric: CLV by regression type: attack-fade bets vs defense-fade bets at various lookback windows (6, 8, 10, 15, 20 matches)
signals.threshold: Optimal attack lookback < optimal defense lookback by 5+ matches; combined CLV > symmetric baseline by +0.5pp
signals.notes: Structural timing signal. If confirmed, changes when the variance-regression filter fires, not just whether it fires. Sweep lookback windows independently for goals-xG (attack) and xGA-conceded (defense) regression candidates.

signals.hypothesis: Using a rolling last-10 window for xGD inputs produces better CLV than an expanding full-season window. Recent form captures tactical shifts and personnel changes that full-season averages dilute.
signals.metric: CLV delta: rolling-10 xGD model vs expanding-window model
signals.notes: Ted frequently cites 'last 10' trends. Test windows: last 6, 8, 10, 15, full season. The solver currently uses full season — this would change data-prep inputs.

signals.hypothesis: CLV increases monotonically with model edge size. There exists a minimum edge threshold below which CLV is zero or negative (vig eats the edge). Mapping the full CLV-vs-edge curve reveals the optimal minimum edge and whether 'large edge = strong bet' holds. Collapses H07 (min edge), H09 (large edge), H10 (no edge) into one test.
signals.threshold: Monotonically increasing CLV across buckets; CLV turns positive at some identifiable threshold
signals.result: CLV is perfectly monotonic: 0-2%→+1.0%, 2-5%→+3.4%, 5-7%→+6.0%, 7-10%→+8.3%, 10-15%→+12.0%, 15%+→+17.8% (N=675,932,472,481,384,119). Model edge estimates are well-calibrated. ROI is NOT monotonic (noisy): +2.6%, +1.8%, +0.7%, +3.8%, +3.2%, +1.1% — expected given sample sizes. CLV turns positive at 0% (all buckets positive), so there is no dead zone. Low-edge bets (0-2%) still show +2.6% ROI on 675 bets.
signals.notes: Calibration test PASSED. Model is well-calibrated — higher edge = higher CLV consistently. Current 5% minEdge is reasonable but 0-2% bets are also profitable. ROI noise at 15%+ (only 119 bets) is sample-size driven. No deployment action needed — this validates the model, not a filter.

signals.hypothesis: When the closing line has moved 0.25+ goals toward the model's position since opening, remaining CLV is near zero. Bets placed after significant line movement toward you should be filtered out.
signals.metric: CLV of bets where line moved 0.25+ toward model vs bets where line was stable or moved against
signals.threshold: Post-move CLV < +1% (confirming value is gone); stable-line CLV > +2%
signals.notes: Requires opening + closing odds data (line-movement.ts signals already built but not registered). Ted's 2nd most referenced signal (151 statements). If confirmed, add as pre-bet filter: skip when sharp money already moved the line your way.

signals.hypothesis: Teams with BOTH attacking overperformance AND defensive underperformance (goals > xG AND goals_conceded > xGA) produce higher CLV when backed than teams with only one variance type. The intersection of H01+H04 is stronger than either component alone because defense regresses more reliably than attack, so net regression favors the team.
signals.metric: CLV of double-variance bets vs single-variance bets
signals.notes: Rare filter — few teams qualify simultaneously. May not reach significance (<50 bets over 3 seasons). If N is too small, mark as inconclusive rather than rejected. Ted described this pattern explicitly for Wrexham/Ipswich/Southampton/Leicester in Mar 2026.

signals.hypothesis: Blending MI model probs with 40% devigged closing market odds improves 1X2 ROI by filtering to higher-quality bets (Benter method — market signal enters twice: once in solver KL-divergence, once in output reweighting)
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta vs pure MI model (--ted --markets=sides --max-odds=2.5)
signals.result: +4.3% ROI (889 bets) vs +2.4% baseline (1456 bets). Hit rate dropped 52.6% → 50.5%. Profit +38.1u vs +34.9u. Bootstrap test FAILED: p=0.093/0.118, CI includes zero. ROI improvement is not statistically significant.
signals.notes: MI model already extracts market signal via KL-divergence minimization. Double-counting at 85% makes model = market (no edge). 40% preserves model signal while adding calibration. Market weight sweep: 10%→+2.1%, 20%→+1.5%, 30%→+2.2%, 40%→+4.3%, 50%→+0.8%. Next: bootstrap significance test.

signals.hypothesis: Blending MI model AH probs with 40% devigged closing AH odds improves AH ROI
signals.metric: backtest ROI delta for AH bets (--ted --markets=ah --max-odds=2.0)
signals.result: +2.7% ROI (452 bets) vs +3.1% baseline (725 bets). Worse across all edge thresholds. Hit rate dropped 54.2% → 51.8%. AH market already efficient enough that blending adds noise, not signal.

signals.hypothesis: Benter Boost at 40% market weight for 1X2-only bets produces statistically significant ROI improvement over baseline (not just noise from fewer bets)
signals.metric: Bootstrap p-value for ROI difference (Benter 40% vs baseline)
signals.result: i.i.d. bootstrap p=0.093 (barely <0.10) but 95% CI [-2.1%, +10.5%] includes zero. Block bootstrap p=0.118 (>0.10), CI [-2.7%, +11.4%]. Permutation p=0.097. DSR verdict: WEAK. Baseline comparison: p=0.182, CI [-2.7%, +7.4%] — also not significant. Neither variant's ROI is distinguishable from noise. Benter trades higher ROI (+4.3% vs +2.4%) for lower CLV (+7.3% vs +9.3%) and 39% fewer bets (889 vs 1456).
signals.notes: Do not deploy Benter Boost for 1X2. The ROI improvement is not statistically significant — CI includes zero and block bootstrap fails p<0.10. The baseline itself isn't significant either (p=0.182), suggesting 1X2 ROI in general needs more data or better filters.

signals.hypothesis: Soccer produces more zeros than standard Poisson predicts due to structural suppression (defensive setups, dead rubbers, tactical stalemates). Adding a zero-inflation parameter to the MI Bivariate Poisson solver improves Brier score on totals markets. ZIP fixes the marginal goal distribution — different from Dixon-Coles tau which fixes correlation structure.
signals.metric: Brier score delta (ZIP vs standard Poisson) on O/U 2.5 markets
signals.threshold: Lower Brier score with ZIP (any improvement)
signals.notes: ZIP ≠ DC tau correction. Tau fixes correlation between home/away goals in low-scoring games. ZIP fixes the marginal distribution itself. Need both. Requires new solver variant with p_zero parameter. Test on totals markets specifically — that's where wrong goal distribution hurts most.

signals.hypothesis: CLV varies significantly by league market efficiency. Softer markets (Serie B, Championship) yield 2-3x the CLV of sharp markets (EPL) because the market is further from the prediction ceiling. This is not a weighting adjustment — it's a capital allocation decision.
signals.metric: CLV per league at edge >= 5%, tested across all available leagues including Serie B and Championship
signals.threshold: At least one league has CLV > 2x the lowest-CLV league
signals.notes: Tested on 4 top leagues: CLV by league × edge bucket shows nearly identical curves (EPL, La Liga, Bundesliga, Serie A all within 0.5pp at every edge level). No soft market effect among top leagues. CANNOT reject or accept until Serie B/Championship solver caches are built. The hypothesis specifically predicts softer leagues have higher CLV — top leagues may all be similarly efficient.

signals.hypothesis: Elo uses W/L/D outcomes only — a strict subset of what Dixon-Coles already sees (goals). Adding Elo to the MI+DC ensemble may dilute signal rather than add orthogonal information. Ensembles only work if models contribute information no individual model knows.
signals.metric: Out-of-sample log loss and CLV: MI+DC vs MI+DC+Elo
signals.threshold: MI+DC+Elo must improve log loss by > 0.005 to justify inclusion
signals.result: Elo ADDS value, does not dilute. At production config (odds≤2.0, edge≥7%, no draws): MI-only=1092 bets/+2.9% ROI, MI+DC=1116/+3.3%, MI+DC+Elo=1217/+3.7%/+45.5u. CLV identical at +11.0% across all three. Elo smooths MI noise → 11% more qualifying bets at same edge quality. Unfiltered: MI-only=-4.8% ROI, MI+DC=-2.7%, MI+DC+Elo=-1.3%. Keep Elo in ensemble.
signals.notes: Tested via --ensemble and --ensemble --no-elo flags in backtest-eval.ts. DC fit on all played matches (static, not walk-forward). Equal-weighted 1/3 consensus.

signals.hypothesis: Market efficiency can be measured per league by regressing final match margin against model spread and Pinnacle spread separately. The regression slope coefficients empirically calibrate Benter weights instead of hand-tuning. A slope near 1.0 = well-calibrated market, <1.0 = overconfident market, >1.0 = underconfident.
signals.metric: R² and slope coefficients per league (model spread vs Pinnacle spread), coefficient variance across leagues
signals.threshold: Slope coefficients differ meaningfully across leagues (range > 0.15)
signals.notes: Current Benter weights are hardcoded in lib/mi-picks/league-config.ts (all leagues same: Market 40%, MI 45%, Elo 15%). Mack's method: one regression per league calibrates all weights at once. Requires new script — zero existing margin regression infrastructure.

signals.hypothesis: The model may have systematic overconfidence or underconfidence bias. If predicted probabilities are off by 5%+, Kelly sizing will be wrong even with positive CLV — the model stakes too much on marginal bets.
signals.metric: Calibration histogram (predicted prob buckets vs actual win rate), mean absolute calibration error, directional bias
signals.threshold: Mean absolute calibration error < 3% (well-calibrated), or identify scalar correction factor
signals.result: CONFIRMED: Model is systematically OVER-CONFIDENT by 6.9%. MACE=6.9% (fails <3% threshold). Every probability bucket shows actual hit rate below predicted: 50-60% bucket predicts 56% but hits 50.3% (-5.9%), 60-70% predicts 65% but hits 56.0% (-7.4%), 70-80% predicts 74% but hits 66.1% (-7.4%). With Kelly sizing this would cause systematic overbetting on marginal bets. A scalar correction of ~0.93x on predicted probabilities would partially fix this.
signals.notes: Mack was right — calibration isn't a dashboard feature, it's a bet-sizing input. Next steps: (1) apply scalar correction to model probs, (2) re-run backtest to measure ROI impact, (3) integrate into picks-engine if positive. The --calibrate flag with shrinkage already exists but uses a different mechanism (shrink toward uninformative prior).

signals.hypothesis: CLV varies by gameweek range due to known distortion windows: early season (team strength uncertain, market relies on stale priors), post-transfer window (squad changes not reflected), manager changes (market over/underreacts), fixture congestion (rotation). Betting more aggressively in high-CLV windows improves ROI.
signals.metric: CLV by gameweek bucket (GW 1-5, 6-10, 11-19, 20-29, 30+)
signals.threshold: At least one bucket has CLV > 1.5x the overall mean (actionable timing edge)
signals.result: CONFIRMED: Massive late-season collapse. GW 6-10: +8.8% ROI (n=57). GW 11-19: +14.4% ROI (n=123). GW 20-29: +16.0% ROI (n=118). GW 30+: -1.3% ROI (n=867). CLV is flat across all windows (~9.1%), but ROI diverges wildly. 60% of bets fall in GW 30+ where the model breaks even. Mid-season (GW 11-29) is the sweet spot: +15.1% ROI on 241 bets vs -1.3% on 867 late-season bets.
signals.notes: CLV doesn't vary by gameweek (all ~9.1%), so the model identifies value consistently. But ROI collapses late season — this suggests late-season variance is higher (dead rubbers, rotation, motivation asymmetry). Actionable: either filter out GW 30+ or reduce stake size. This alone could flip overall ROI from +2.4% to much higher. Needs bootstrap significance test on the GW 30+ subset.

signals.hypothesis: Filtering out bets after GW 29 improves ROI by removing the late-season collapse window where dead rubbers, rotation, and motivation asymmetry cause high variance despite stable CLV.
signals.metric: ROI delta: --skip-late=29 vs baseline (--ted --markets=sides --max-odds=2.5)
signals.threshold: Filtered ROI > baseline ROI by +3pp AND bootstrap p < 0.10
signals.result: ROI: +13.9% vs +2.4% baseline (+11.5pp improvement). P&L: +41.6u vs +34.9u (more profit on 298 vs 1456 bets). Bootstrap: i.i.d. p=0.005, block p=0.005, 95% CI [+3.3%, +24.8%] EXCLUDES ZERO. Permutation p=0.008 z=2.37. Hit rate: 58.7% vs 52.6%. CLV stable: +9.0% vs +9.3%. DSR still WEAK (144 trials). La Liga underperforms at -17.2% ROI (n=51) — small sample.
signals.notes: Strongest significance result we've seen. The filter removes 80% of bets but increases both ROI and total profit. Caution: (1) only 298 bets — smaller sample, (2) La Liga is negative, (3) DSR flags multiple testing. Consider deploying as soft filter (reduced stake rather than skip) for GW 30+. The 2023-24 season shows +27.7% ROI which may be inflating the aggregate.
